Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That Matters In Request For Admissions, Set No. 1 Be Deemed Admitting And Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Patrina Harrison, In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON'S Motion For An Order That Matters In Request For Admissions, Set No. 1 Be Deemed Admitting And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Patrina Harrison, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Jennifer Meikle, of YOKA | SMITH, LLP, (213) 427-2300, for Defendants.

At the outset, the court notes that Plaintiff's objection to the court's tentative ruling is untimely and therefore, argument will not be allowed.

After brief reply from Plaintiff, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion is denied. Service of substantially code-compliant responses to requests for admission moots the motion to deem requests admitted. Request for sanctions is also denied as moving party is self-represented. The Court also notes that neither party complied with local rules applicable to discovery motions, including captioning papers and courtesy copies. Parties are reminded to adhere to all applicable rules, including applicable local rules.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
